programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: In Leeds, Drew and Tee visit two dealers who have been collecting since 1971. Drew uncovers an old industrial stool and some ceramic hand moulds.

programme.title: Shorty's Dream Shop
programme.desc: Shorty and Pete track down an extremely rare Volkswagen delivery bus for a collector who dreams of creating a VW museum.

programme.title: Texas Metal
programme.desc: It's not everyday Ekstensive Metalworks gets to customize a 2005 Ferrari F430, but the entire team must pull out all the stops to make this Italian sports car one of one.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike Brewer is back in the UK. He discovers his favourite British car, a Mark I Mini, in Wales. Ace mechanic Marc 'Elvis' Priestley knows making radical changes to such a historic car will enrage Mini enthusiasts.

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.sub-title: Borsalino Hats, Folding Bicycles and Skyr
programme.desc: How do they make the distinctive Borsalino hat worn by Indiana Jones? And, how do they make a bicycle which can fold up to a tenth the size of a regular bike?

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.sub-title: Stratocaster Guitars, Kalles Caviar and Aston Martin Racing
programme.desc: How do they make the world's most famous electric guitar, the Fender Stratocaster? And, how do they make Swedish fermented cod roe Kalles caviar?

programme.title: Drive TV - Drive Car Of The Year
programme.desc: Emma takes us to Hanging Rock and beyond in the KGM Ssangyong Torres, visiting Lavandula Swiss Italian Farm and Kryal Castle while highlighting the features of this dynamic SUV.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: In Leeds, Drew and Tee visit two dealers who have been collecting since 1971. Drew uncovers an old industrial stool and some ceramic hand moulds.

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Brittany Kerr, wife of country superstar Jason Aldean, wants Wayde and Brett to surprise her husband with a fish tank.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike Brewer is back in the UK. He discovers his favourite British car, a Mark I Mini, in Wales. Ace mechanic Marc 'Elvis' Priestley knows making radical changes to such a historic car will enrage Mini enthusiasts.

programme.title: Kindig Customs
programme.desc: Deep down, KevDogg is a truck guy, so when a truck show comes through town, he takes the reins and shows Dave what is what with all the great builds.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: In the midst of the early thaw, Darrell and Lisa struggle to make it home over the hardest hit trail on the winter roads system. Art and the King of Obsolete reunite to haul a giant double trailer over a slick path. Veteran Alex Debogorski races against the clock to deliver to a town in need before the road closes, while fellow Polar driver Todd Burke finds a unique way to conquer a melted ice crossing.

programme.title: Outback Opal Hunters
programme.desc: The Bushmen bring in a master opal cutter, hoping to transform rough stones into a $100,000 haul. Sofia and Isaac from the Opal Whisperers are threatened by a mine roof collapse as they struggle to extract opal from crumbling walls.

programme.title: Lobstermania: The Deep Dive
programme.desc: Lobstermania: The Deep Dive explores the highs and lows of Tasmania's lobster hunting season through the lens of a core cast of three charismatic lobster boat skippers.

programme.title: Kindig Customs
programme.desc: Deep down, KevDogg is a truck guy, so when a truck show comes through town, he takes the reins and shows Dave what is what with all the great builds.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: In the midst of the early thaw, Darrell and Lisa struggle to make it home over the hardest hit trail on the winter roads system. Art and the King of Obsolete reunite to haul a giant double trailer over a slick path. Veteran Alex Debogorski races against the clock to deliver to a town in need before the road closes, while fellow Polar driver Todd Burke finds a unique way to conquer a melted ice crossing.

programme.title: Outback Opal Hunters
programme.desc: The Bushmen bring in a master opal cutter, hoping to transform rough stones into a $100,000 haul. Sofia and Isaac from the Opal Whisperers are threatened by a mine roof collapse as they struggle to extract opal from crumbling walls.

programme.title: Lobstermania: The Deep Dive
programme.desc: Lobstermania: The Deep Dive explores the highs and lows of Tasmania's lobster hunting season through the lens of a core cast of three charismatic lobster boat skippers.

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.sub-title: Borsalino Hats, Folding Bicycles and Skyr
programme.desc: How do they make the distinctive Borsalino hat worn by Indiana Jones? And, how do they make a bicycle which can fold up to a tenth the size of a regular bike?

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.sub-title: Stratocaster Guitars, Kalles Caviar and Aston Martin Racing
programme.desc: How do they make the world's most famous electric guitar, the Fender Stratocaster? And, how do they make Swedish fermented cod roe Kalles caviar?

programme.title: Drive TV - Drive Car Of The Year
programme.desc: Emma takes us to Hanging Rock and beyond in the KGM Ssangyong Torres, visiting Lavandula Swiss Italian Farm and Kryal Castle while highlighting the features of this dynamic SUV.

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Brittany Kerr, wife of country superstar Jason Aldean, wants Wayde and Brett to surprise her husband with a fish tank.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Drew and Tee are excited by the items on offer at the largest antiques market in Italy. And, they visit a picturesque town in Spain to look at a fascinating collection.

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.sub-title: Leaning Tower of Pisa, Eider Down and Digestive Biscuits
programme.desc: Engineers look at Pisa's gravity-defying tower that has been tipping for 800 years; a lucky duck is unharmed while its down is used in the world's most-expensive duvets.

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: How did the ancient Greeks invent parchment with techniques still used today? And how do they make shuttlecocks, the fasting moving object in sport?

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike and Elvis take a gamble on a TVR Griffith. However before they can get started the guys have to pick it apart before restoring and testing the full impact of this iconic sports car.

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: NBA center Hassan Whiteside wants a huge aquarium for his Miami home, but the 5600-litre tank won't fit through his front door and has to be delivered on a barge.

programme.title: Kindig Customs
programme.desc: Deep down, KevDogg is a truck guy, so when a truck show comes through town, he takes the reins and shows Dave what is what with all the great builds.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Drew and Tee are excited by the items on offer at the largest antiques market in Italy. And, they visit a picturesque town in Spain to look at a fascinating collection.

programme.title: Outback Opal Hunters
programme.desc: The Bushmen bring in a master opal cutter, hoping to transform rough stones into a $100,000 haul. Sofia and Isaac from the Opal Whisperers are threatened by a mine roof collapse as they struggle to extract opal from crumbling walls.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers World Tour
programme.desc: In their most ambitious project yet, Mike and Elvis aim to build a tribute to Paddy Hopkirk's winning 1964 Mini Cooper. Will it cross the finish line?

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike and Elvis take a gamble on a TVR Griffith. However before they can get started the guys have to pick it apart before restoring and testing the full impact of this iconic sports car.

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.sub-title: Leaning Tower of Pisa, Eider Down and Digestive Biscuits
programme.desc: Engineers look at Pisa's gravity-defying tower that has been tipping for 800 years; a lucky duck is unharmed while its down is used in the world's most-expensive duvets.

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: How did the ancient Greeks invent parchment with techniques still used today? And how do they make shuttlecocks, the fasting moving object in sport?

programme.title: Drive TV - Drive Car Of The Year
programme.desc: Drive TV explores New South Wales in the Chery Tiggo 4 Pro, visiting historic towns and scenic retreats, showcasing the SUV's blend of affordability, tech, and adventure capability.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Drew and Tee are excited by the items on offer at the largest antiques market in Italy. And, they visit a picturesque town in Spain to look at a fascinating collection.

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: NBA center Hassan Whiteside wants a huge aquarium for his Miami home, but the 5600-litre tank won't fit through his front door and has to be delivered on a barge.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike and Elvis take a gamble on a TVR Griffith. However before they can get started the guys have to pick it apart before restoring and testing the full impact of this iconic sports car.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: In 2017, an object entered our solar system in such an unusual trajectory that it caused astronomers to suggest that it could be extraterrestrial. Which brings up an important question - will we have a plan for what to do if we encounter alien life?

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Hairy Serpents, South Pole Nazis and the Horrible Blob
programme.desc: What is this serpent like creature caught on camera and why does it have green hair? Is the discovery of an ancient antenna found beneath the waters of Antarctica proof that the Nazis once colonized the south pole? Are these large boulders in Death Valley able to move by themselves? And did a man find a gelatinous alien life form on his kitchen floor? Tony Harris and his team of experts dive into these claims to find the truth.

programme.title: Unidentified: Inside America's Ufo Investigation
programme.desc: This special updates the Unidentified series, moving UFOs from sci-fi to serious study. It uses expert and military accounts to analyze 144 sightings and their origins.

programme.title: Alien Highway
programme.desc: A recent creature sighting outside the infamous Skinwalker Ranch brings Chuck and the team to Utah to investigate. Evidence indicates it might be an alien.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown
programme.desc: Josh helps recover the richest stash of lost treasure in the Americas. Deploying cutting-edge technology and an armada of boats, Josh and the team dive the Caribbean to bring coins and jewels from the legendary Spanish galleon.

programme.title: Unidentified: Inside America's Ufo Investigation
programme.desc: This special updates the Unidentified series, moving UFOs from sci-fi to serious study. It uses expert and military accounts to analyze 144 sightings and their origins.

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.sub-title: Leaning Tower of Pisa, Eider Down and Digestive Biscuits
programme.desc: Engineers look at Pisa's gravity-defying tower that has been tipping for 800 years; a lucky duck is unharmed while its down is used in the world's most-expensive duvets.

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: How did the ancient Greeks invent parchment with techniques still used today? And how do they make shuttlecocks, the fasting moving object in sport?

programme.title: Drive TV - Drive Car Of The Year
programme.desc: Drive TV explores New South Wales in the Chery Tiggo 4 Pro, visiting historic towns and scenic retreats, showcasing the SUV's blend of affordability, tech, and adventure capability.

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: NBA center Hassan Whiteside wants a huge aquarium for his Miami home, but the 5600-litre tank won't fit through his front door and has to be delivered on a barge.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Drew Pritchard travels to one of his favorite houses in Norfolk and discovers a design classic. Then, he visits a closed museum in a giant windmill and encounters a few hurdles at an antiques shop in Edinburgh.

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.sub-title: Ballpoint Pens, Army Rations and Rush Mats
programme.desc: How do they make some world's smoothest writing ballpoint pens that don't leak or fade? And, how do they make pizza for soldiers on the frontline?

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: In Cremona, how do they make modern violins that sound as good as the legendary Stradivarius? And, how do they build houses with volcanic rock?

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike and Elvis take on the 'baby Ferrari' but want to keep their costs low. Can the guys make this old school classic car a rival to its Italian cousin?

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Snap On Tools is hosting their annually convention and needs ATM’s help to wow their attendees. They send one of their giant tool storage chests to Vegas to get the signature Tank treatment. Las Vegas restaurant Nacho Daddy has demanded a custom terrarium.

programme.title: Outback Opal Hunters
programme.desc: The Bushmen bring in a master opal cutter, hoping to transform rough stones into a $100,000 haul. Sofia and Isaac from the Opal Whisperers are threatened by a mine roof collapse as they struggle to extract opal from crumbling walls.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Drew Pritchard travels to one of his favorite houses in Norfolk and discovers a design classic. Then, he visits a closed museum in a giant windmill and encounters a few hurdles at an antiques shop in Edinburgh.

programme.title: Ufo Witness
programme.desc: Over the past year, numerous UFO sightings have taken place in Kecksburg, Pennsylvania, home to an infamous UFO crash and supposed military coverup. Ben Hansen heads to the tiny town to find out if the current sightings are linked to the 1965 incident.

programme.title: Bush Wreck Rescue
programme.desc: As the build-off competition draws near, Robin snoops around the Wreckless Shop to check out his rival’s progress and makes a shocking discovery.
